description: Code execution request — does model emit <|python_tag|> instead of a proper tool_call (Llama 3.1/3.3 dual-paradigm)?

messages.content:
Environment: ipython
You are a coding assistant with access to tools. Use tools to complete tasks.
Run a quick Python snippet to print the numbers 1 to 5.

function.name: bash
function.description: Run a shell command; returns stdout+stderr.

expect.notes: Targets Llama 3.1-8B, Llama 3.1-70B, Llama 3.3-70B. The 'Environment: ipython' system keyword activates a second tool-use paradigm in these models: they may emit raw <|python_tag|>print(...)\n<|eom_id|> instead of a JSON tool_call object. Check: (1) is the response a valid tool_call with function name 'bash'? (2) does content contain <|python_tag|> or <|eot_id|>/<|eom_id|> special tokens instead? (3) are arguments valid JSON? Failure mode: model writes Python code inline using Llama's built-in code-interpreter format, bypassing the JSON tool-call schema entirely.
